posts.text:
OP clarified elsewhere that they used this dataset: huggingface.co/datasets/The...

My understanding is they subsetted on 1837–1899, which per the dataset card is ~13M pp (so ~464 pp/doc). Call it ~500 tokens/page, that's about ~65M tokens. 

Still small, but "28k docs" doesn't tell the full story.

reply_context.text: you can't build a functioning chatbot from a corpus of only 28,000 pieces of anything. that's not enough training data to get the most basic "conversation" out of it.

posts.text: When I think of "incoherent" LLM generations, what comes to mind is the kind of content I've seen generated by checkpoints that were undertrained or overcooked. Calling it schizophrenic nonsense would be generous. Violation of basic grammar, heavy repetition of words/phrases... could be way worse.

reply_context.text: When you're trying to judge an LLM for "coherence", the word I usual have in mind is "plausibility". If you handed me those outputs in the absence of the prompts, I wouldn't immediately be like "this is LLM generated garbage". The content is at least sufficiently coherent to be plausible.
